up.units: g/L (time course only, no rate value)
up.method: DNS (dinitrosalicylic acid) assay; consumption curve in Fig 1B; no numeric rate reported

growth_records.cite: Zhang, Xiangmei et al. Suitable extracellular oxidoreduction potential inhibit rex regulation and effect central carbon and energy metabolism in Saccharopolyspora spinosa. Microbial Cell Factories (2014). DOI:10.1186/s12934-014-0098-z. [PMC4172946]
growth_records.snippet: The total yield of spinosad and PSA under oxidative condition reached 308 mg/L, which was 3.11 fold of that in control group ... Dry cell weight (DCW) under oxidative condition was increased slightly, less than 4%.
growth_records.notes: Batch shake-flask fermentation of wild-type S. spinosa, CONTROL condition. No specific growth rate, uptake rate or secretion rate reported numerically; only DCW/glucose/product time-courses (Fig 1). Control spinosad+PSA final titer derived: oxidative = 308 mg/L = 3.11-fold control, so control = 308/3.11 = 99.04 mg/L (arithmetic shown). This is a final product TITER, not a secretion rate. Temperature not stated for S. spinosa fermentation (E. coli cloning at 37 C only). pH 7.2 (fermentation medium). Full medium composition stated. NADH/NAD+ ratio measured: ~0.31 (exp phase 24-48 h), 0.52 at 72 h, >0.52 in stationary control.

growth_records.strain: ATCC 49460 (wild-type), oxidative condition (5 mmol/L H2O2 added every 12 h from 72 h)

up.units: g/L (no numeric rate; consumption similar to control per Fig 1B)
up.method: DNS assay; no numeric rate reported

growth_records.cite: Zhang, Xiangmei et al. Suitable extracellular oxidoreduction potential inhibit rex regulation and effect central carbon and energy metabolism in Saccharopolyspora spinosa. Microbial Cell Factories (2014). DOI:10.1186/s12934-014-0098-z. [PMC4172946]
growth_records.snippet: The total yield of spinosad and PSA under oxidative condition reached 308 mg/L, which was 3.11 fold of that in control group.
growth_records.notes: WT S. spinosa OXIDATIVE condition (H2O2 pulsed). Measured spinosad+PSA final titer = 308 mg/L (HPLC), 3.11-fold higher than control; DCW increased <4% vs control. Glucose consumption 'did not have difference' vs control (no numbers). No growth rate/uptake/secretion RATE reported. Titer is not a rate. NADH/NAD+ dropped to 0.28-0.32 (stationary) under H2O2. Temperature not stated.

growth_records.strain: ATCC 49460 (wild-type), reductive condition (3 g/L DTT added at 72 h)

up.units: g/L (glucose consumption RATE increased vs control, qualitative; no number)
up.method: DNS assay; Fig 1B shows increased consumption rate, no numeric value

sec.units: mg/L (decreased significantly vs control; no number given)

growth_records.cite: Zhang, Xiangmei et al. Suitable extracellular oxidoreduction potential inhibit rex regulation and effect central carbon and energy metabolism in Saccharopolyspora spinosa. Microbial Cell Factories (2014). DOI:10.1186/s12934-014-0098-z. [PMC4172946]
growth_records.snippet: Glucose consumption rate under reducing condition was increased ... In contrast, the yield of spinosad and PSA under reducing condition was decreased significantly.
growth_records.notes: WT S. spinosa REDUCTIVE (DTT) condition. Cell growth showed no difference vs control. Glucose consumption RATE increased (qualitative, no number). Spinosad+PSA yield decreased significantly (no number). No quantitative rates extractable. Included for completeness of distinct condition; low confidence due to absence of numeric values.

growth_records.strain: Lu106 (rex-deletion mutant, ATCC 49460 background), control condition

sec.units: mg/L (severely decreased vs WT; no number given)

growth_records.cite: Zhang, Xiangmei et al. Suitable extracellular oxidoreduction potential inhibit rex regulation and effect central carbon and energy metabolism in Saccharopolyspora spinosa. Microbial Cell Factories (2014). DOI:10.1186/s12934-014-0098-z. [PMC4172946]
growth_records.snippet: the growth of S. spinosa Lu106 exhibited a growth defect relative to that of the wild type ... The yield of spionsad and PSA in rex mutant was severely decreased.
growth_records.notes: rex-deletion mutant Lu106, control condition. Growth defect + delayed stationary entry vs WT (qualitative, Fig 1A). Spinosad+PSA severely decreased (no number). NADH/NAD+ stable and low throughout fermentation. No numeric growth/uptake/secretion rate. Included as a distinct genotype condition; low confidence due to absence of numeric values.
